title: Fire Escape Voicemail
image_prompt: Horizontal cinematic 16:9 still frame on a narrow urban fire escape at dusk. A human in their late twenties stands with a phone in hand, halfway between anger and grief, trying to say something honest into a voice memo and failing. Anky stands beside them in a worn jacket, blue skin and purple hair grounded in the real world, not fantasy. Brick walls, window reflections, city glow below, cigarette butts in a tray, humid summer evening. The emotion is about choosing honesty over pride. Cinematic realism, intimate body language, strong facial expression, subtle wind, deeply human tension.
video_prompt: Animate this as a 15-second horizontal 16:9 scene with a clear emotional arc. Start with the human pacing the fire escape and stopping the recording twice. In the middle, Anky gently reaches out, lowers the phone a little, and gives them a look that says say the real thing. End with the human finally pressing record, speaking from the chest instead of the ego, and softening with relief. Use realistic dusk city ambience, slight handheld camera movement, meaningful pauses, no spectacle.

title: Bakery Opening Bell
image_prompt: Horizontal cinematic 16:9 still frame inside a small neighborhood bakery before opening. Flour dust in the morning light, trays of pastries, handwritten menu board, half-tied apron. A human owner looks terrified that nobody will come. Anky stands close by helping adjust the apron and steady the moment. The scene is about courage before being seen. Warm ovens, practical details, street visible through the front window, cozy realism, Anky feels embodied and like a true companion rather than a mascot.
video_prompt: Create a 15-second horizontal 16:9 clip with a clear interaction and payoff. Begin with the owner frozen near the front door, staring at the closed sign. In the middle, Anky ties the apron properly, nudges the sign to open, and the bell above the door rings. End with the first customer stepping in, the owner's face shifting from fear to grounded joy, and Anky sharing a small satisfied smile. Camera should hold on hands, faces, and the bell moment. Human warmth, commercial-ready clarity, real bakery movement.

title: Empty Apartment First Night
image_prompt: Horizontal cinematic 16:9 still frame in a nearly empty apartment on move-in night. Stacked boxes, one lamp, keys on the floor, city light through bare windows. A human sits cross-legged on the hardwood, overwhelmed by the loneliness of starting over. Anky kneels beside an open box that contains a kettle, two mugs, and a framed photograph. The feeling is not sadness alone but the first moment of making a life again. Grounded realism, vulnerable stillness, subtle hope, cinematic domestic detail.
video_prompt: Animate this as a 15-second horizontal 16:9 scene with a beginning, middle, and end. Start with the human staring at the boxes like the room is too empty to bear. In the middle, Anky opens the box, finds the kettle and photo, and starts making the space feel lived in by setting down two mugs and placing the photo on the floor against the wall. End with the human laughing softly through tears, getting up, and opening the next box with intention. Quiet apartment ambience, warm lamp glow, very human pacing.

title: Boxing Gym Corner
image_prompt: Horizontal cinematic 16:9 still frame in an amateur boxing gym after a rough sparring round. Sweat, ring ropes, scuffed floor, old posters, corner stool, wraps half undone. A human boxer sits frustrated and ashamed, convinced they are not built for this. Anky crouches in front of them, rewrapping their knuckles with calm precision. The scene is about learning that composure beats force. Real gym texture, no fantasy effects, emotionally legible faces, Anky feels solid, practical, and deeply present.
video_prompt: Generate a 15-second horizontal 16:9 clip with a strong action arc. Begin with the boxer ripping at their wraps in frustration and looking away from the ring. In the middle, Anky stops their hands, rewraps the knuckles carefully, demonstrates one small footwork shift with a grounded gesture, and gets eye contact back. End with the boxer standing, breathing differently, stepping toward the ring with renewed clarity instead of rage. Use realistic gym sound, slight camera sway, and clear emphasis on hands, breath, and posture.

title: Recording Booth First True Take
image_prompt: Horizontal cinematic 16:9 still frame in a compact recording studio. Vocal booth glass, microphone, lyric sheets, headphones, dim amber practical lights. A human musician is stuck because the song becomes too personal when they sing it honestly. Anky stands on the other side of the glass near the control desk, one hand near the talkback button, fully tuned in. The scene is about dropping performance and allowing real feeling into the work. Tactile studio realism, intimate eye contact through glass, emotionally charged stillness.
video_prompt: Animate this into a 15-second horizontal 16:9 clip with a precise emotional beat. Start with the musician attempting a line, stopping, and looking frustrated in the booth. In the middle, Anky presses the talkback button, says nothing performative, just gives one grounding gesture to sing it like you mean it. End with the musician closing their eyes, stepping back to the mic, and delivering the first true line of the song while Anky listens with total stillness. Emphasize the red recording light, breath before the take, and the moment truth enters the room.

title: Thrift Store New Self
image_prompt: Horizontal cinematic 16:9 still frame in a thrift store fitting area. Mirrors, clothing racks, patterned carpet, fluorescent light softened by late afternoon sun from the storefront. A human stands in a coat they secretly love but feel afraid to become. Anky adjusts the collar and studies them in the mirror with warmth and precision. The scene is about identity, permission, and stepping into a truer version of yourself. Real retail textures, modern human fashion, emotionally clear expressions, grounded urban realism.
video_prompt: Create a 15-second horizontal 16:9 clip with a clear transformation arc. Begin with the human staring at themselves in the mirror, almost about to take the coat off. In the middle, Anky steps in, straightens the collar, turns them gently back toward the mirror, and holds the gaze with them instead of letting them look away. End with the fitting room curtain opening, the human stepping out with quiet confidence, and a friend or shop clerk reacting with delight. Use subtle mirror choreography, real store ambience, and a meaningful shift from hesitation to embodiment.

title: Shelter Adoption Moment
image_prompt: Horizontal cinematic 16:9 still frame inside a dog shelter visiting room. A shy older dog stands a few feet away while a human kneels, unsure whether they are ready to be responsible for love again. Anky kneels nearby with one hand resting softly on the floor, making the space feel safe. The room is simple and real: water bowl, worn chair, leash hook, late afternoon light through wired glass. The emotional meaning is about risking tenderness again after loss. Cinematic realism, compassionate faces, grounded atmosphere.
video_prompt: Animate this as a 15-second horizontal 16:9 scene with a clear relational payoff. Start with the human hesitating and keeping their hands close to their body while the dog watches cautiously. In the middle, Anky lowers to the floor, makes the energy gentle, and encourages patience rather than reaching too fast. End with the dog finally crossing the room and placing its head into the human's hand, the human breaking into a real smile, and Anky witnessing the exact second their heart opens again. No melodrama, just precise tenderness and believable animal motion.

title: Wedding Speech Side Room
image_prompt: Horizontal cinematic 16:9 still frame in a side room off a wedding reception. String lights outside the door, sound of distant laughter, suit jacket over a chair, handwritten note cards in shaking hands. A human is panicking because they want to give a speech that actually means something, not just a safe joke. Anky stands beside them adjusting a crooked tie and holding their focus. The moment is about speaking love truthfully in public. Elegant but grounded event realism, emotionally specific faces, warm practical light.
video_prompt: Generate a 15-second horizontal 16:9 clip with a strong before-and-after. Begin with the human fumbling the note cards and rehearsing something hollow. In the middle, Anky steadies their hands, straightens the tie, and silently pushes them toward one line that matters. End with the doors opening, the human stepping into the reception, lifting the glass, and speaking with genuine emotion while the room quiets to listen. Capture the threshold moment, the breath before walking out, and the shift from performance to real love.
